commands.review_summary:
The branch changes Antigravity quota summary labeling, compact icon lane selection, highest-usage provider ranking, and focused tests to use rendered Gemini and Claude/GPT 5-hour and weekly lanes.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main still restricts Antigravity compact quota-summary rendering to Gemini buckets while highest-usage ranking consumes that rendered resolver, and the PR tests cover Claude/GPT-constrained lanes.
commands.review_followup: - No repair lane is needed because the PR already contains a focused implementation, tests, and sufficient real UI proof; the remaining action is normal maintainer review and merge handling.

target.title: Fix Antigravity quota summary lanes
target.body: ## Summary - rank Antigravity automatic menu bar usage from the rendered 5-hour and weekly quota lanes across both model groups - ignore non-renderable quota cadences when choosing the automatic highest-usage provider - keep Antigravity compact icon rendering aligned with the same most-constrained rendered lanes - shorten Antigravity quota lane titles to match the new grouped Google UI (`Gemini 5-hour`, `Claude/GPT weekly`, etc.) - update focused regression tests for the new quota grouping behavior ## Verification - `swift test --filter 'CodexbarTests|AntigravityQuotaSummaryTests|MenuBarMetricWindowResolverTests|UsageStoreHighestUsageTests'` - `swift test --skip-build --filter antigravity` - `make check` - manually launched the patched app and verified the menu shows the new grouped lanes without raw localization keys ## Screenshot Evidence - Source UI: Google Antigravity settings now groups quota into Gemini Models and Claude/GPT models, each with weekly and 5-hour limits. - Verified UI: CodexBar now renders `Gemini 5-hour`, `Gemini weekly`, `Claude/GPT 5-hour`, and `Claude/GPT weekly` with correct remaining percentages. Note: local screenshot files were used for verification but are not committed to the repo.
target.author: yuxin-qiao
